name: Overcoming the Monster
description: A protagonist confronts and defeats a powerful antagonist or existential threat that endangers a community or world.

nodes.label: Peaceful State Under Threat
nodes.definition: A community or world exists in relative peace, but a monstrous force threatens to destroy it. The protagonist is part of or connected to what is threatened.
entry_conditions: The audience sees both the world worth saving and the shadow of the threat
exit_conditions: The monster's threat becomes immediate and undeniable
typical_variants: Idyllic community with a distant menace
World already under partial siege
Protagonist unaware of the threat's true scale
failure_modes: Community is so underdeveloped that its destruction wouldn't matter
Threat is mentioned but not felt
signals_in_text: Small signs of the monster's influence

nodes.label: Monster Revealed
nodes.definition: The monster's nature, power, and threat become clear. The community and protagonist understand what they face.
entry_conditions: The monster acts or is discovered in a way that cannot be ignored
exit_conditions: The protagonist accepts the need to confront the monster
typical_variants: First attack on the community
Discovery of the monster's lair or nature
Witness to the monster's destructive power
failure_modes: Monster is revealed but seems beatable without effort
signals_in_text: Destruction or death caused by the monster

nodes.label: Call to Confront
nodes.definition: The protagonist accepts the mission to face the monster, either by choice, duty, or necessity. Others may refuse or be unable.
entry_conditions: No one else can or will act
exit_conditions: The protagonist is committed to the confrontation
typical_variants: Volunteering when others won't
Being the only one with a specific capability
Forced by circumstance — the monster targets the protagonist personally
failure_modes: Commitment is forced so completely that the character has no agency
No reason this specific character must be the one to act

nodes.label: Preparation and Reconnaissance
nodes.definition: The protagonist gathers intelligence, allies, weapons, or skills needed to face the monster. The monster's weakness or pattern is studied.
entry_conditions: The protagonist lacks something needed for the confrontation
exit_conditions: The protagonist has what they need (or believes they do) and moves toward the monster
typical_variants: Studying the monster's weakness
Building a weapon or trap
Training or acquiring a skill
Recruiting a specialist
failure_modes: Preparation gives the hero everything — no remaining uncertainty
Preparation is skipped, making victory feel unearned

nodes.label: Initial Encounter
nodes.definition: The protagonist engages the monster for the first time in a meaningful way. This encounter reveals the monster's true power and the protagonist's inadequacy.
entry_conditions: The protagonist enters the monster's domain or the monster comes to them
exit_conditions: The protagonist survives but realizes the task is harder than expected
typical_variants: First battle that goes badly
Failed trap that only angers the monster
Narrow escape that reveals a vulnerability
failure_modes: Initial encounter is too easy — no sense of the monster's superiority
Protagonist is defeated so thoroughly there's no plausible recovery
signals_in_text: The plan fails or partially succeeds

nodes.label: Nightmare Phase
nodes.definition: The monster demonstrates its full power. The protagonist suffers losses, setbacks, or revelations that make victory seem impossible.
entry_conditions: Initial encounter has shown the monster's strength
exit_conditions: The protagonist discovers or earns the key insight needed for the final confrontation
typical_variants: Allies killed or captured
Monster attacks the community directly
Protagonist's confidence shattered
Discovery that the monster has a deeper nature
failure_modes: Nightmare is one-note — just more destruction without character impact
No new information about the monster emerges

nodes.label: Final Confrontation
nodes.definition: The protagonist faces the monster in the decisive battle. Victory requires exploiting the discovered weakness and accepting personal risk or sacrifice.
entry_conditions: The protagonist has nothing left to try except the final confrontation
exit_conditions: The monster is defeated or the protagonist fails
typical_variants: Direct combat using the discovered weakness
Outwitting the monster through a trap
Sacrifice that destroys both monster and something precious
Turning the monster's power against itself
failure_modes: Victory comes from a power not previously established
Monster is defeated too easily given prior buildup
Victory requires no sacrifice or cost

nodes.label: World Restored
nodes.definition: With the monster defeated, the threatened community or world is safe. The protagonist is recognized, mourned, or changed by the confrontation.
typical_variants: Community celebration and recognition of the hero
Quiet restoration with scars remaining
Hero's sacrifice is honored
The world is safe but forever changed
failure_modes: Restoration is too neat — the monster's destruction had no lasting impact
Hero's cost is ignored in the celebration

edges.label: Threat Strikes
edges.preconditions: Peaceful state is established; the monster acts or is discovered
edges.effects_on_stakes: The community's survival is now in question
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist is forced to confront the existence of the threat
edges.common_alternatives: Monster has been present all along but its true nature is only now revealed
edges.anti_patterns: Monster appears with no prior hints or foreshadowing

edges.label: Accepts the Mission
edges.preconditions: The monster's threat is clear; someone must act
edges.effects_on_stakes: The protagonist's personal survival is now tied to the community's fate
edges.effects_on_character: Transitions from witness to combatant; accepts the mantle
edges.common_alternatives: Others push the protagonist to act; protagonist is the only option
edges.anti_patterns: Commitment is costless — the protagonist has nothing to lose

edges.label: Gathers Tools and Knowledge
edges.preconditions: Commitment is made; the protagonist recognizes they are not yet ready
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each preparation step raises hope but also reveals the scale of the task
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist grows in capability and understanding of the monster
edges.common_alternatives: Preparation is compressed into a single discovery; preparation spans multiple locations
edges.anti_patterns: Preparation solves everything — no remaining uncertainty

edges.label: First Engagement
edges.preconditions: Preparation is done; the protagonist moves to engage
edges.effects_on_stakes: The theoretical threat becomes viscerally real
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist's confidence is tested; often humbled
edges.common_alternatives: The monster comes to the protagonist rather than the reverse
edges.anti_patterns: First encounter goes well — no sense of the monster's superiority

edges.label: Overwhelmed
edges.preconditions: Initial encounter has shown the protagonist's inadequacy
edges.effects_on_stakes: Everything worsens — the monster retaliates and the community suffers
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist must dig deeper; questions whether victory is possible
edges.common_alternatives: Nightmare is triggered by the monster's retaliation for the initial encounter
edges.anti_patterns: Nightmare phase is just more of the same without escalation or new information

edges.label: Key Insight Gained
edges.preconditions: The nightmare reveals something new about the monster or the protagonist
edges.effects_on_stakes: A path to victory is visible but narrow and costly
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist refocuses — the approach changes based on new understanding
edges.common_alternatives: Insight comes from an ally, a prior clue, or the monster's own behavior
edges.anti_patterns: Insight is a convenient power-up with no narrative basis

edges.label: Monster Falls
edges.preconditions: The final confrontation is resolved; the monster is defeated
edges.effects_on_stakes: Existential threat is removed; the community can rebuild
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist carries the cost of the confrontation; is changed by it
edges.common_alternatives: Monster is contained rather than destroyed; victory is pyrrhic
edges.anti_patterns: Restoration erases all consequences; the world is exactly as it was
